title: Responsive site design system deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because A cross-page design system requires multi-file consistency and usability review beyond one bounded browser artifact.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Responsive site design system: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? A design system is complete once colors and spacing variables are defined.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.
A design system is complete once colors and spacing variables are defined.
